title: Turnkey Secures $12.5M for Secure Digital Asset Cloud
summary: Turnkey has raised $12.5 million to develop and launch its Verifiable Cloud, a secure computing product for digital assets. The funding round was backed by Circle Ventures and Sequoia Capital, highlighting growing interest in secure digital asset solutions.
body:
Turnkey, a company specializing in secure computing for digital assets, has raised $12.5 million in a funding round led by Circle Ventures and Sequoia Capital. The new capital will primarily fund the development and public launch of Turnkey Verifiable Cloud, a product designed to provide secure computing environments for digital assets.

The Verifiable Cloud aims to address the growing need for secure and verifiable computing solutions in the digital asset space. This product will allow users to perform complex computations on digital assets while ensuring the integrity and security of the data. The funding round underscores the confidence that major investors have in Turnkey's vision and technology.

For everyday users, this development means more secure options for managing and transacting digital assets. As digital assets become more integrated into everyday financial activities, having secure computing solutions will be crucial. This could lead to broader adoption of digital assets in various applications, from personal finance to enterprise solutions.

Investors and users should watch for the public launch of Turnkey Verifiable Cloud, which is expected to bring new security standards to the digital asset market. The involvement of major investors like Circle Ventures and Sequoia Capital suggests that this product could set new benchmarks in secure computing for digital assets.
